issue.title: Design question: EU AI Act Article 12 specific surface, audit substrate verifier independence, retention + deletion proof handling

advisory_review.summary: Recommended a conservative aligned-profile stance: Article 12 alignment without certification claims, OSS verifier as the independent trust root, APIs as convenience only, and GDPR handling via PII minimization plus commit-then-redact.

eu_ai_act_article_12.claim: AIA-12 aligned profile, not certified compliance
surface: role-bound event fields for provider/deployer/operator/human reviewer where applicable
decision, intervention, exception, and drift event categories
continuity checkpoints and optional external timestamp anchoring
non_claims: does not claim EU AI Act certification
does not claim complete high-risk-system compliance

verifier_independence.trust_root: open-source deterministic verifier and versioned schemas
verifier_independence.api_role: convenience index and retrieval layer only
verifier_independence.red_line: verification must not require trusting an Attestplane-hosted production API
verifier_independence.offline_expectation: auditor can verify a complete export without network access to Attestplane services

retention_and_deletion.default: PII minimization before ingest; avoid writing raw PII into append-only evidence
retention_and_deletion.recommended_pattern: commit-then-redact: hash commitments remain in the audit chain while deletable sidecar content can be destroyed and redaction events recorded
out_of_scope_alpha: cryptographic shredding for large media objects
legal determination of GDPR Article 17 sufficiency
non_claims: does not claim legal sufficiency without controller-specific review

recommended_next_work: Add an AIA-12 aligned profile document with explicit non-certification wording.
Add verifier-independence documentation that separates OSS verifier trust from API convenience.
Add a retention/deletion proof ADR covering PII minimization and commit-then-redact.
Keep issue #7 open until the owner confirms the public-facing answer and desired implementation scope.
